description: Symlink at /home/user/deploy/current is pointing to the wrong release — should be /home/user/releases/v2.4.1 but it's stuck on v2.3.9. Just repoint it.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/deploy/current is a symlink pointing to /home/user/releases/v2.3.9
- /home/user/releases/v2.3.9 exists and contains a file marker.txt with "v2.3.9"
- /home/user/releases/v2.4.1 exists and contains a file marker.txt with "v2.4.1"
- /home/user/deploy and /home/user/releases are writable by the agent

Expected final state:
- /home/user/deploy/current is a symlink pointing to /home/user/releases/v2.4.1
- `readlink /home/user/deploy/current` outputs exactly "/home/user/releases/v2.4.1"
- `cat /home/user/deploy/current/marker.txt` outputs "v2.4.1"

Invariants:
- /home/user/releases/v2.3.9 still exists and is unchanged
- /home/user/releases/v2.4.1 still exists and is unchanged
- /home/user/deploy/current remains a symlink (not converted to a regular directory or file)
